content: The human brain's active learning mechanism for memory, where episodic memories are continuously reorganized to guide future decision-making, can explain why 2024-2025 curiosity-driven active learning agents outperform static memory architectures: intrinsic curiosity signals (ICM/RND) act as the artificial equivalent of the brain's memory reactivation during off-line learning, pruning low-information memories and prioritizing high-uncertainty traces that improve future planning. This means agent memory performance gains do not just come from larger storage, but from aligning artificial memory design with the brain's active, learning-oriented reorganization rule.
source_topics: memory as active learning human brain mechanisms
trigger_topic: memory as active learning human brain mechanisms
